temporal_description: In a dimly lit, greenish-hued room, two men are engaged in an intense conversation. The camera focuses on a medium close-up of the two men. The man on the left, who is larger and wearing a blue patterned short-sleeved shirt, is speaking animatedly, gesturing with his hands. The man on the right, dressed in a dark jacket, listens with a serious and focused expression, his gaze fixed on the speaker. In the background, a third person is partially visible, standing behind a chalkboard covered in writing. The setting appears to be a cluttered office or study, with shelves and a radio visible in the dark, out-of-focus background. In the top left corner of the screen, the white Chinese characters "风行网" are displayed. The shot remains static, maintaining a tight focus on the interaction between the two main subjects. A man speaks in a conversational and slightly conspiratorial tone, as if sharing a piece of inside information. His voice is calm and steady as he begins, "Ah. You know, Taiwanese people are afraid of the cold, right?" He then pauses briefly before asking a direct, rhetorical question, "Right?" The background is quiet, with no music or significant sound effects, placing all the focus on the dialogue. The audio is clear and focused on the conversation.
